events.description: Israeli Air Force launches 'wide-scale' strikes on Tehran using approximately 80 fighter jets dropping 230 munitions. Targets include IRGC Aerospace Force headquarters, oil storage depots at Shahran, Aghdasieh, and Karaj, and the Tehran refinery. 16 cargo aircraft used by IRGC Quds Force destroyed at Mehrabad Airport. At least 4 killed. First strikes on Iran's oil infrastructure since war began.

Tehran engulfed in toxic black smoke after overnight Israeli strikes on fuel depots cause fires to burn for hours. Iran's Red Crescent warns residents to stay indoors due to acid rain containing hydrocarbons, sulphur and nitrogen oxides. Oil-tainted black rainfall reported across the city causing chemical burn risk.

Israeli Navy strikes Ramada hotel in Beirut's Rawche seaside area, killing 5 senior IRGC Quds Force commanders including Majid Hassini (Lebanon Corps finance), Ali Reza Bi-Azar (Lebanon Corps intelligence chief), Ahmad Rasouli (Palestine Corps intelligence chief), and Hossein Ahmadlou (Lebanon Corps intelligence). 4 killed per Lebanese health ministry, 10 wounded.
events.source: Times of Israel, France 24, Al Jazeera

events.description: Iran's Assembly of Experts elects Mojtaba Khamenei (57), son of slain Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, as the third Supreme Leader of the Islamic Republic. IRGC and senior officials pledge allegiance. Trump calls him 'lightweight' and demands input in choosing Iran's leader. IDF issues statement in Persian warning it will 'pursue every successor.'
events.source: NPR, NBC News, CBS News, Al Jazeera

events.description: Iranian ballistic missile with cluster warhead strikes central Israel, hitting Tel Aviv and Petah Tikva. 6-7 wounded including a man in his 40s in serious condition in Tel Aviv. Multiple fragment impact sites reported. Sixth Iranian salvo of the day targeting Israel.
events.source: Jerusalem Post, Haaretz, Times of Israel

events.description: Iranian missile strikes residential area in Al-Kharj governorate, Saudi Arabia, killing 2 foreign workers (Indian and Bangladeshi nationals) and wounding 12 Bangladeshi residents. First civilian fatalities in Saudi Arabia since the war began. Saudi defense ministry reports intercepting 15 Iranian drones near Riyadh.

Pentagon confirms 7th US service member killed in the conflict. The soldier died from injuries sustained during an Iranian attack on a base in Saudi Arabia on March 1. Total US military deaths now at 7 since Operation Epic Fury began.

Iranian drone attack damages water desalination plant in Bahrain and causes fire at Mina Salman seaport facility. 3 people injured. Attack raises fears about targeting of civilian water infrastructure in Gulf states.

UAE reports new Iranian barrage of 17 ballistic missiles and 117 drones. UAE air defense intercepts 16 missiles; 17th falls into sea. Combined with earlier wave of 15 ballistic missiles and 119 drones intercepted on March 7. Debris and shrapnel from interceptions cause injuries in populated areas. Total UAE casualties since war began: 4 killed, 112 injured.
events.source: Gulf News, The National, Al Jazeera

events.description: At least 41 killed and 40 wounded in Israeli air-and-ground raids on Nabi Chit (Nabi Sheet) in Lebanon's eastern Bekaa Valley. Israeli helicopter-borne commandos land in the town in an overnight operation reportedly aimed at locating remains of missing IAF navigator Ron Arad. Fierce ground battles reported between IDF and Hezbollah.
events.source: Al Jazeera, France 24, Middle East Monitor

events.description: Israel's strikes on 30 Iranian fuel depots go 'far beyond' what the US expected, sparking the first significant US-Israel disagreement since the war began. US officials express concern about escalation and environmental damage. Oil prices reach $100/barrel for first time since July 2022.

UN declares the war a 'major humanitarian emergency' as nearly 25 million people in affected regions are already refugees, internally displaced, or recent returnees. UNHCR mobilizes across region. 517,000+ displaced in Lebanon; 84,000 in collective shelters. Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed, disrupting humanitarian supply routes to Gaza, Yemen, and Somalia.

Reports confirm China secretly supplied Iran with approximately $5 billion in weapons via an oil-for-weapons deal, including HQ-9B surface-to-air missile systems, HQ-16B SAMs, FN-6 MANPADS, CM-302 anti-ship missiles, JY-26 radar systems, and Sunflower-200 kamikaze drones. The deal was signed covertly and accelerated after the June 2025 Israel-Iran 12-day war.
events.source: Global Defense Corp, The National, Times of Israel, Jerusalem Post

events.description: Chinese-made HQ-9B air defense systems deployed around Tehran fail to intercept US-Israeli strikes during opening hours of Operation Epic Fury. US/Israeli forces suppressed Iranian radar and data links first, rendering the SAMs ineffective. Iran's multi-layered air defense — including Chinese HQ-9B, Russian S-300, and domestic systems — was overwhelmed within hours. Analysts say the failure should 'worry the Chinese Communist Party' about their export weapons' combat performance.
events.source: The Economist, India TV News, Global Defense Corp, People's News Today

metadata.note: Latest conflict updates - Day 9-10 events (March 7 evening through March 8, 2026). All events sourced from verified major news outlets. No confirmed new events found specifically for March 9 beyond continuation of existing operations. The database existing files cover through March 8 morning; these entries fill in major verified events from March 7 evening through March 8.

countries_actively_involved: United States (Operation Epic Fury - airstrikes on Iran)
Israel (Operation Roaring Lion - airstrikes on Iran, Lebanon ground invasion)
Lebanon/Hezbollah (strikes on Israel, ground fighting with IDF)
Saudi Arabia (targeted by Iran, intercepting drones/missiles)
UAE (targeted by Iran, air defense operations, considering strikes on Iranian missile sites)
Bahrain (targeted by Iran, hosting US Fifth Fleet)
Iraq (Iranian proxy activity, US base attacks)
Cyprus (RAF Akrotiri drone strike from Hezbollah)
Russia (intelligence sharing with Iran per reports)
major_developments_last_24h: Mojtaba Khamenei named new Supreme Leader of Iran, replacing his slain father
Israel strikes Iranian oil infrastructure for first time - 30+ fuel depots hit in Tehran area
Tehran engulfed in toxic smoke and acid rain from burning fuel depots
First US-Israel disagreement as oil strikes exceed agreed scope
7th US service member dies from injuries sustained in Saudi Arabia attack
First Saudi civilian deaths (2 killed in Al-Kharj)
5 IRGC Quds Force commanders killed in Israeli strike on Beirut hotel
Iran fires cluster warhead missile at central Israel, 6-7 wounded
41 killed in Israeli raids on Nabi Chit in Bekaa Valley, Lebanon
Iranian desalination plant strike in Bahrain raises civilian infrastructure fears
conflict_status_summary.strait_of_hormuz_status: Effectively closed. Near-total halt in maritime traffic since Iran's IRGC warned it would attack any vessel attempting passage. About 20% of global oil supply normally transits the strait. 400+ ships stuck. No oil shipments in past 24 hours. Insurance companies have withdrawn coverage for the region.
conflict_status_summary.diplomatic_efforts: Stalled. Trump demands Iran's 'unconditional surrender' before negotiations. UN Security Council held emergency session with Russia and China calling for immediate ceasefire. Oman (former mediator) condemned strikes as undermining active negotiations. UK urges Iran to return to dialogue. No ceasefire in sight.
conflict_status_summary.humanitarian_situation: UN declares 'major humanitarian emergency.' 25 million people in affected areas already refugees/displaced. 517,000+ displaced in Lebanon. 22 million need humanitarian support. 17 million face food insecurity. Strait of Hormuz closure disrupts aid deliveries to Gaza, Yemen, Somalia. Tehran residents warned to stay indoors due to toxic air from burning fuel depots. 32,000 Americans evacuated from Middle East since Feb 28.
